questions.question: Which observation best proves that matter is made of extremely small particles?
questions.explanation: Sugar dissolves because its tiny particles spread throughout the water.

questions.question: Which statement correctly explains why the volume of water does not increase significantly when sugar dissolves?

options.B: Sugar particles occupy spaces between water particles

questions.explanation: Sugar particles fit into the spaces between water particles.

questions.question: Why are solids difficult to compress?

options.B: Particles have very little space between them

questions.explanation: In solids, particles are tightly packed with minimal space between them.

questions.question: Which property of gases allows them to spread rapidly in air?

options.C: Large spaces and rapid particle motion

questions.explanation: Gas particles move rapidly and have large spaces between them.

questions.question: Which statement best explains the difference between solids and liquids at the particle level?

options.A: Liquids have stronger attraction than solids
options.C: Liquid particles move around each other while solid particles vibrate in fixed positions

questions.explanation: Solid particles vibrate in place while liquid particles move around each other.

questions.question: Why does diffusion occur faster in gases than in liquids?

options.B: Gas particles move faster and are farther apart

questions.explanation: Greater spacing and faster motion allow quicker diffusion in gases.

questions.question: Which factor mainly increases the rate of diffusion in liquids?
questions.explanation: Higher temperature increases particle kinetic energy and motion.

questions.question: In the syringe experiment, why does the plunger move inward when pressed?
questions.explanation: Gas particles have spaces between them and can be compressed.

questions.question: Which process involves a change in particle arrangement but not chemical composition?
questions.explanation: Melting is a physical change where particle arrangement changes.

questions.question: Why does evaporation occur at temperatures below boiling point?

options.B: Surface particles gain enough energy to escape

questions.explanation: Surface particles with enough energy escape into vapor form.

questions.question: Which property best distinguishes gases from liquids?

options.C: Gases expand to fill entire container

questions.explanation: Gas particles move freely and occupy all available space.

questions.question: Which phenomenon demonstrates diffusion in everyday life?

options.B: Perfume smell spreading across a room

questions.explanation: Perfume smell spreads due to diffusion of gas particles.

questions.question: Which change occurs when solid ice becomes water?

options.A: Particles move farther apart and move more freely

questions.explanation: During melting, particles gain energy and move more freely.

questions.question: Which state of matter has particles with the highest kinetic energy?
questions.explanation: Gas particles move fastest and have highest kinetic energy.

questions.question: Why does the smell of food spread quickly in a warm kitchen?
questions.explanation: Higher temperature increases particle motion and diffusion.

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes particle arrangement in liquids?

options.B: Particles move freely but remain close together

questions.explanation: Liquid particles are close but can move around each other.

questions.question: Which property explains why gases have low density?
questions.explanation: Large spaces between gas particles reduce density.

questions.question: Which condition increases the rate of evaporation?
questions.explanation: High temperature and wind increase evaporation rate.

questions.question: Which statement explains why liquids are called fluids?
questions.explanation: Liquids can flow and take the shape of their container.

questions.question: Which particle property determines the physical state of matter?
questions.explanation: Forces and spacing determine whether matter is solid, liquid, or gas.

questions.question: Why does gas exert pressure on container walls?
questions.explanation: Gas pressure results from particle collisions with container walls.

questions.question: Which example shows diffusion between two gases?
questions.explanation: Smoke mixes with air through diffusion of gases.

questions.question: What happens to interparticle attraction when matter changes from solid to gas?
questions.explanation: Attractive forces weaken as particles move farther apart.

questions.question: Which concept explains why solids retain their shape?

options.C: Strong attraction holding particles in fixed positions

questions.explanation: Strong forces keep particles fixed in solids.

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes the particle model of matter?

options.A: Matter is continuous and has no particles
options.B: Matter consists of particles with spaces and forces between them

questions.explanation: Particle theory explains matter as tiny particles with spaces and forces between them.
